label: Experimental setup and PB frame
caption: Experimental configuration. (a) Schematic of the PB-based antibubble generation apparatus including the 3D-printed triangular-prism frame, syringe pump, liquid pool, high-speed camera, and backlight. (b) Top view of the upper PB node showing the three converging soap films (width w = 90 um, angle = 109.5 deg). (c) Photograph of the setup enclosed in the vacuum chamber.

label: Droplet-PB interaction stages and outcomes
caption: Image sequences of droplet-PB interaction. (a) The four universal stages: free fall, entering the PB at the upper node, transport within the PB channel, and release as a packed droplet. The 1G1L multi-layer structure forms at pinch-off (t = 26.7 ms). (b)-(d) Three typical outcomes: (b) Packing (multi-layer formation), (c) Outer coalescence (film rupture before pinch-off), (d) Inner coalescence (collapse during transport).

label: We-Bo phase diagram
caption: We-Bo phase diagram. (a) All 199 experiments at atmospheric pressure. The criterion We + 2Bo = 0.038 (solid line) separates packing outcomes (filled circles, above the line) from insufficient deformation (open triangles, below). The dashed line shows the equivalent criterion for the horizontal liquid-film method (We + 2Bo = 0.072). (b) Experiments with varying ambient pressure P = 0.1-1.0 atm, showing the emergence of pressure-dependent failures (blue circle) not captured by the energy criterion alone.

label: t/t criterion and combined phase diagram
caption: Characteristic time ratio criterion. (a) Schematic of the gas film between the droplet and the deforming soap film in polar coordinates. Lubrication theory applies because the film thickness (epsilon ~ 10 um) is much smaller than the droplet radius (R ~ 1 mm). (b) Combined phase diagram in (We + 2Bo, t_p/t_d) space. The white region satisfies both the We-Bo energy criterion and the t/t drainage criterion, containing 97% of all successful packing outcomes.
